Support Your Local First Responders

Honoring the brave men and women who keep our communities safe in Shelby, NC and beyond

View Statistics Local Organizations

The Reality of First Responder Service

National statistics highlighting the challenges and sacrifices our first responders face daily

~60

Officers Killed Annually

On average, 50-60 law enforcement officers are killed in the line of duty each year in the United States (FBI LEOKA data)

~1,500

Officer Shootouts/Year

Approximately 1,500 police officers are involved in shooting incidents annually, facing life-threatening situations

25-30%

PTSD Among Officers

Studies indicate 25-30% of police officers experience symptoms of PTSD due to traumatic events on duty

~60

Firefighter Deaths/Year

Approximately 60 firefighters die in the line of duty annually in the US (NFPA data)

~65,000

Firefighter Injuries/Year

Over 65,000 firefighters are injured on the job each year, with thousands suffering serious injuries

70%

Volunteer Firefighters

Nearly 70% of firefighters in the US are volunteers, serving their communities without compensation

12+ Hours

ER Shifts During Crisis

ER nurses and doctors routinely work 12+ hour shifts, often extending to 16+ hours during emergencies and pandemics

35%

ER Nurse Burnout

Studies show 35% of ER nurses experience burnout syndrome due to high-stress environments and long hours

30%

Paramedic PTSD

Approximately 30% of paramedics and EMTs experience PTSD, higher than the general population

Shelby, NC First Responder Organizations

Local organizations serving and protecting our community

👮

Police Departments

Shelby Police Department

Providing law enforcement services to the City of Shelby, ensuring public safety and community protection

Address: 126 W Warren St, Shelby, NC 28150

Emergency: 911 | Non-Emergency: (704) 484-4848

Cleveland County Sheriff's Office

County-wide law enforcement serving Cleveland County with patrol, investigations, and detention services

Address: 100 Justice Pl, Shelby, NC 28150

Emergency: 911 | Non-Emergency: (704) 484-4888

🚒

Fire Departments

Shelby Fire Department

Professional fire protection and emergency response services for the City of Shelby

Address: 201 S Lafayette St, Shelby, NC 28150

Emergency: 911 | Non-Emergency: (704) 484-6866

Cleveland County Volunteer Fire Departments

Multiple volunteer fire stations throughout Cleveland County providing fire protection and rescue services

Including: Fallston, Kings Mountain, Boiling Springs, and others

🚑

Paramedics & EMS

Cleveland County EMS

Providing advanced life support and emergency medical services throughout Cleveland County

Emergency: 911 | Administration: (704) 484-4848

Atrium Health Cleveland EMS

Hospital-based emergency medical services supporting the region with rapid response capabilities

Part of Atrium Health Cleveland network

🏥

Emergency Medical Staff

Atrium Health Cleveland Emergency Department

24/7 emergency care provided by dedicated doctors, nurses, and medical professionals

Address: 201 Fallston Rd, Shelby, NC 28150

Emergency: (704) 481-5000

Carolinas HealthCare System Shelby

Comprehensive emergency services with trauma care and specialized medical response teams

Serving Shelby and greater Cleveland County

Support Resources

Crisis hotlines, support organizations, and resources for first responders and their families

📞 Crisis & Support Hotlines

988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline

24/7 free and confidential emotional support

988lifeline.org

Safe Call Now

24/7 helpline for first responders and families

1-206-459-3020

Frontline Helpline

24/7 support by experienced first responders

1-866-676-7500

Responder Health Peer Advocate

Peer support for first responders

(253) 243-3701

🤝 Support Organizations

NC DPS Responder Assistance Initiative

Mental health and wellness resources for NC first responders

Visit Website →

Gary Sinise Foundation

Supporting first responders with equipment, training, and grants

Visit Website →

Duty to Act Coffee Co.

Supporting first responders through community and coffee

Visit Website →

ArmorUp America

Tools, research, and services for frontline workers

Visit Website →

Supporting our community's heroes through technology and awareness
